content: Removed 2 dead IPC handlers (get-home-dir, read-file) + matching preload exposures (readFile, getHomeDir) — zero renderer callers. Build green. Rest of src/main/index.ts and src/preload/index.ts already tight (no v4 leftovers, no commented blocks, no dedup targets). Follow-ups: env.d.ts drift in renderer (Renderer-UX), possibly-unused observability IPC (cross-zone decision), sessionCapture.ts duplicate-key warning (Zone-Runtime-Fleet). Full report at ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md.

CLAUDE.md scrubbed against kill list + 3 zones' drift reports. Edits: (1) IPC surface now reads 'readDir / openDirectory' (dropped readFile/getHomeDir, source: self); (2) removed mailbox-listen.sh from design-goal paragraph + 'No drain loop' note (kill list); (3) trimmed v4 deliveryWarningMs/taskTimeoutMs sentence from Harness timeouts (kill list); (4) trimmed v4 'finished' from Lifecycle state machine (kill list); (5) rewrote VS Code/xterm paragraph — dropped session.tail + 'screen-grid/sigil regex' references (Dispatch-Coord report + kill list); (6) deleted both mailbox/scripts rm -rf bullets (Flow B step 4 + Ephemeral storage). Verified zero residue via grep; AGENTS.md symlink → CLAUDE.md intact. Follow-ups (4): dispatchContext IPC drop, behavior.mode UX dead-code call, env.d.ts drift, sessionCapture duplicate-key warning. Full report at ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md.

Canvas audit complete (read-only, architect-canvas.json untouched). Per-component: Electron Platform zone, Main Process, Canvas Store all accurate; IPC Bridge has 1 drift — its specs string still lists 'readFile' which was removed in t-cleanup-platform. All 7 edges incident to the zone (Desktop App→IPC Bridge, IPC Bridge→Main Process, Main Process→Canvas Store, Main Process→PTY Orchestrator, Dispatch/Terminal/Assistant Panel→IPC Bridge) match real call paths. Edits needed: yes, one — drop 'readFile' from ipc-bridge.data.specs. No other deleted features lingering. Findings appended to ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md under '## Canvas audit (t-canvas-platform)'.
